title: trisetum spicatum subsp. pilosiglume
intro: Trisetum spicatum subsp. pilosiglume is a subspecies of the Trisetum spicatum plant, commonly known as Spear-grass or Barren Brome, a type of grass native to the temperate regions of the world. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Trisetum spicatum subsp. pilosiglume include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
